Key Words.question: What is an atheist?
options: A person who does not believe in God
A person who believes in God
A person who is unsure whether God exists
A person who believes in many gods
Key Words.answer: A person who does not believe in God

Key Words.question: What is a theist?
options: A person who believes in God
A person who does not believe in God
A person who is unsure whether God exists
A person who follows no religion
Key Words.answer: A person who believes in God

Key Words.question: What is an agnostic?
options: Someone who is not sure whether God exists
Someone who believes in many gods
Key Words.answer: Someone who is not sure whether God exists

Key Words.question: What does polytheism mean?
options: Belief in more than one God
Key Words.answer: Belief in more than one God

Key Words.question: What does 'follower' mean in religion?
options: A person who belongs to or follows a religion
A person who does not study
Key Words.answer: A person who belongs to or follows a religion

Problem of Evil.question: What is moral evil?
Problem of Evil.answer: Evil caused by human actions

Problem of Evil.question: Which is an example of moral evil?
Problem of Evil.answer: Murder

Problem of Evil.question: What is natural evil?
options: Evil caused by events outside human control
Problem of Evil.answer: Evil caused by events outside human control

Problem of Evil.question: Which is an example of natural evil?
Problem of Evil.answer: Hurricane

Problem of Evil.question: What is the Problem of Evil?
options: The question of how evil can exist if God is all-powerful, all-knowing and all-loving
The question of why churches have windows
The question of who founded Sikhi
The question of why people celebrate Christmas
Problem of Evil.answer: The question of how evil can exist if God is all-powerful, all-knowing and all-loving

Problem of Evil.question: Which three words are often linked to the Problem of Evil?
Problem of Evil.answer: Omnipotent, omniscient and omnibenevolent

Problem of Evil.question: Why might evil make some people question God?
options: They wonder why a loving and powerful God allows suffering
They do not know what a font is
They think all religions began in India
Problem of Evil.answer: They wonder why a loving and powerful God allows suffering

Problem of Evil.question: What might some atheists argue about evil?
options: Evil is the same as Christmas
Problem of Evil.answer: Evil means God cannot exist

Problem of Evil.question: What might some theists argue about evil?
options: Evil can test faith or be part of God's plan
Evil proves there is no religion
Evil was invented by Henry Tudor
Problem of Evil.answer: Evil can test faith or be part of God's plan

Problem of Evil.question: Which explanation might some Christians give for evil?
options: It may be caused by the devil
It is caused by stained glass windows
It is caused by the Golden Temple
Problem of Evil.answer: It may be caused by the devil

Problem of Evil.question: Which explanation might some Christians give for suffering?
options: It may be a test of faith
It proves churches are not special
It means no one should help others
It means all religions are polytheistic
Problem of Evil.answer: It may be a test of faith

Problem of Evil.question: How might humanists respond to suffering?
options: Try to improve the lives of people who are suffering
Pray only at the Golden Temple
Problem of Evil.answer: Try to improve the lives of people who are suffering

Problem of Evil.question: Which statement best describes moral evil?
options: Humans have a choice and cause the action
Humans have no control over it
It is always caused by weather
It only happens in ancient stories
Problem of Evil.answer: Humans have a choice and cause the action

Problem of Evil.question: Which statement best describes natural evil?
options: It happens because of events humans cannot directly control
It is always caused by a human choice
It means believing in many gods
Problem of Evil.answer: It happens because of events humans cannot directly control

Problem of Evil.question: Why is theft an example of moral evil?
options: A person chooses to do it
Problem of Evil.answer: A person chooses to do it

Problem of Evil.question: Why is an earthquake an example of natural evil?
options: It is not caused by a person's choice
It is always caused by murder
Problem of Evil.answer: It is not caused by a person's choice

Problem of Evil.question: What does omnibenevolent suggest God should want to do?
options: Love people and want good for them
Problem of Evil.answer: Love people and want good for them

Problem of Evil.question: What does omnipotent suggest God should be able to do?
options: Stop evil because he is all-powerful
Problem of Evil.answer: Stop evil because he is all-powerful

Problem of Evil.question: What does omniscient suggest God would know?
options: That evil and suffering are happening
Problem of Evil.answer: That evil and suffering are happening

Problem of Evil.question: Why is the Problem of Evil difficult for some believers?
options: It challenges belief in a loving, powerful and knowing God
It is only about church furniture
It proves all religions are the same
Problem of Evil.answer: It challenges belief in a loving, powerful and knowing God
